refined_fr.refined_fr: Derive, record, and manage performance requirements (e.g., cycle time, energy efficiency, availability) from worksite mission context to ensure traceability and historical calibration.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR is broad and involves multiple activities: derivation, recording, and management of performance requirements. Refining clarifies these distinct sub-functions to enable targeted design and verification.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify by demonstrating documented performance targets traceable to worksite inputs and a maintained history of baseline and calibration updates.
sub_frs: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context
Record derived performance requirements with traceability
Manage performance requirement history including baselines and calibration updates

candidate_dps: Distributed performance data collection and aggregation
Performance requirements database with version control
selected_dp.selected_dp: Centralized performance requirements derivation engine integrated with a version-controlled performance database

potential_sources: Tight coupling between derivation logic and data storage
Interface dependencies with worksite mission context inputs
coupling_check.notes: The centralized engine logically separates derivation and management but requires well-defined interfaces to worksite data and calibration inputs to avoid excessive coupling.

assumptions_risks: Assumes reliable and timely access to worksite mission context data
Risk of performance bottleneck if centralized engine is overloaded
High safety criticality demands rigorous validation of derivation logic
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider modularizing derivation and management functions if coupling or performance issues arise.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: Overall system-level module responsible for performance requirement derivation, recording, and management

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives real-time worksite mission context and equipment operational data

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives mission task definitions and updates

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Supports data exchange with external calibration and baseline update sources

module_containment_scores.notes: High containment as this module logically owns performance requirement derivation and management; low diffusion as functionality is centralized.
Vehicle module has limited direct involvement; risk of diffusion if performance data is duplicated here.
Minimal containment; potential diffusion risk if teleoperation logs performance data independently.
Safety Services primarily focused on alerts; low containment but risk of diffusion if performance requirements overlap safety parameters.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Centralizing performance requirement derivation and management within NGHE Solution minimizes diffusion and supports traceability.
containment_risks: Potential for performance data duplication in Vehicle or Teleoperation modules
Interface complexity with external calibration sources via Network Link
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Enforce strict interface contracts and data ownership policies to prevent diffusion across modules.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The selected DP logically satisfies the refined FR sub-functions with clear traceability and manageable coupling.

diffusion_risks: Possible duplication of performance data in Vehicle and Teleoperation modules
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Centralizing in NGHE Solution module provides strong containment; interface discipline is critical.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification can be performed by reviewing documented performance targets and history within NGHE Solution module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of responsibility to NGHE Solution module with defined external interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Define strict interface protocols to prevent data duplication
Monitor performance of centralized engine to avoid bottlenecks

decision.justification: The current level of decomposition, DP selection, and IM assignment meet the requirements and constraints with acceptable risk and verification feasibility.
next_level_sub_frs: Derive performance requirements from worksite mission context
Record derived performance requirements with traceability
Manage performance requirement history including baselines and calibration updates

revise.why: No immediate revisions required; proceed to next decomposition level.

assumptions: Reliable access to worksite mission context data
Centralized engine can handle expected data volume
open_risks: Data duplication risk in other modules
Interface complexity with external calibration sources
evidence_needed: Verification test plans for performance requirement traceability
